channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas to release 3 male hostages, Israel to release dozens of Palestinian prisoners under ceasefire
item.description: Hamas-led militants are set to release three male hostages from the Gaza Strip on Saturday, in return for the release of dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of a fragile ceasefire with Israel that has halted more than a year of intense fighting and has so far seen 15 hostages and hundreds of pri
item.dc:creator: Waafa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages
item.description: Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages despite international pressure on the military four years after it seized power from an elected civilian government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 6 American hostages in Venezuela freed after Trump envoy meets with Maduro
item.description: Six Americans who were being held hostage in Venezuela were freed Friday night following President Trump’s envoy for special missions Richard Grenell's visit to the South American country. "Just been informed that we are bringing six hostages home from Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Polus, The Hill

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Colombian president urges undocumented Colombians in US to return
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro urged undocumented Colombians who are living in the U.S. to return home, saying they would be rewarded for it. "I ask undocumented Colombians in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban take over Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel, more than a decade after attacking it
item.description: The Taliban are taking over the operations of Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel in Kabul, more than a decade after they launched a deadly attack there that killed nine people. The Serena Hotel said Friday it was closing its operations in the Afghan capital on Feb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Italian court blocks asylum-seekers expulsions in challenge to Meloni’s migrant deal with Albania
item.description: An Italian appeals court has refused to approve the speedy expulsion of 43 asylum seekers detained in Albania under a controversial migration deal to move the proceedings beyond European Union borders.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: The U.N. says Rwanda-backed rebels were quickly expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing the region’s major city.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months have freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met with a senior Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano And Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Grenell to give Venezuela a warning from Trump administration
item.description: President Trump’s envoy for special missions, Richard Grenell, is delivering an in-person message to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro that there will be consequences if detained Americans are not released and Venezuelan “criminals and gang members” deported from the U.S. are not repatriated.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Turkey dismisses 5 military academy graduates for taking discontinued secular oath
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Five military academy graduates and three of their immediate superiors were dismissed from the Turkish Armed Forces for taking a pro-secular oath during their graduation ceremony, the defense ministry announced on Friday The Ministry of National Defense launched a disciplinary
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Panama president: 'Impossible' to negotiate over canal
item.description: Panama President José Raúl Mulino rejected negotiations over ownership of the Panama Canal, in the face of President Trump’s fixation on the U.S. retaking the trade waterway.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Three Greek opposition parties vowed Friday to challenge the country’s center-right government with a censure motion over its handling of a deadly rail disaster nearly two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released Saturday, including the father of the youngest captives held in Gaza, brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Muslims attend annual Biswa Ijtema event in Bangladesh
item.description: TONGI, Bangladesh (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered on a riverbank near Bangladesh’s capital on Friday to listen to sermons by Islamic scholars at the Biswa Ijtema, or global congregation of Muslim devotees.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian anti-graft protesters march to a northern city and plan to block bridges
item.description: Hundreds of students have marched through the Serbian countryside, taking their anti-graft protest to the northern city of Novi Sad, where they plan to blockade three bridges over the River Danube this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Eldar Emric, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostages' families welcome news that 3 more men will be released
item.description: The Israeli organization representing families of hostages in Gaza has celebrated the news that three high-profile men will be released next.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo say they plan to take their fight to the capital
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels who captured eastern Congo's largest city said Thursday they want to take their fight to the far-off capital, Kinshasa, while Congo’s president called for a massive military mobilization to resist the rebellion and his defense minister rejected calls for talks
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar’s military government extends its mandate to rule another 6 months
item.description: Myanmar’s military government has extended its mandate to rule another six months, as the country enters its fifth year of crisis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After the initial ecstasy of freedom, released hostages face a long road to recovery
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Ilana Gritzewsky returned to Israel after being held captive in Gaza for 55 days in November 2023, she had so much adrenaline coursing through her body she couldn’t sleep for two days. “You don’t understand that it’s really over," Gritzewsky recalled.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fear, disease and debt afflict Venezuelans released from prison after post-election arrests
item.description: TOCUYITO, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro labeled them as terrorists on national television. They were plucked from pharmacies, apartment buildings and other locations, and thrown in prison for months.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's ban on UN's Palestinian aid agency has come into effect. Here's what that looks like
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday formally banned the main United Nations aid agency for Palestinian refugees from operating on its territory despite heavy diplomatic backlash, a move that humanitarian officials warn could have a disastrous impact on aid delivery and jeopardize regional stability
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband, imprisoned for raping and drugging her, now caught up in other cases
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Dominique Pélicot, the convicted rapist who horrified France by drugging his then wife so other men could rape her, was questioned Thursday about other cases of rape and murder that he's suspected in.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu furious about chaotic handover of Israeli hostages from Hamas
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered a delay in the planned release of terrorists, seeking assurance regarding the release of hostages, but the prime minister's office later indicated that the demand had been satisfied.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu delays Palestinian prisoner release after 'chaotic' Hamas scenes
item.description: Israel will delay the release of Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails amid chaotic scenes surrounding Hamas's release of hostages it kidnapped from Israel and held since its attack on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Chaos and uncertainty swirl around Trump's foreign aid freeze
item.description: President Trump’s decision to clean house at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) and initially freeze all foreign assistance is fueling chaos and uncertainty in Washington and across the world.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman used wigs, disguises to take British citizenship tests for at least 14 people, authorities say
item.description: A British woman used wigs and disguises to impersonate applicants taking citizenship tests across the United Kingdom, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio exempting 'life-saving humanitarian assistance’ from foreign aid restrictions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio issued a waiver on Tuesday exempting “life-saving humanitarian assistance” from President Trump’s executive order pausing nearly all foreign aid during a period of review. Trump’s executive order pausing funding, issued on his first day in office, suspended any new U.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
